source_paper_title: Load Balancing in Parallel Queues and Rank-Based Diffusions
context:
Page 14, end of Section 3.3 (following Remark 3.8, just before Section 3.4 Organization)

Lastly, we note that although the case \(b = aK\) is not investigated here, we believe that the average long time behavior of the corresponding MJSQ system should resemble the unstable case (Theorem 3.7) where \(b > aK\). This is because, although the lowest queue will become empty infinitely often, the time spent at zero will be small and thus the average boundary effect of the origin will diminish over time.
title: Critical-case asymptotics of MJSQ when the marginal-bias parameter satisfies \(b=aK\)
latex:
Consider \(K\ge 2\) parallel single-server queues with exponential service times of rate \(n\) at each server and a central dispatcher with Poisson arrivals of rate \(nK-\upsilon\sqrt{n}\), where \(n\in\mathbb{N}\) is a heavy-traffic parameter and \(\upsilon\in\mathbb{R}\). Under the marginal join-the-shortest-queue (MJSQ) policy, assume parameters \(a>0\) and \(b>0\) with
\[a_1=a-b,\qquad a_i=a\ \text{ for } i=2,\dots,K,\qquad \upsilon=aK-b.\]
Let \(X_i^n(t)\) denote the \(i\)-th shortest (ranked) queue length at time \(t\ge 0\) (ties broken lexicographically), and define the diffusion-scaled gap process \(\hat Z^n(t)\in\mathbb{R}_+^K\) by
\[\hat Z_1^n(t)=\frac{X_1^n(t)}{\sqrt{n}},\qquad \hat Z_i^n(t)=\frac{X_i^n(t)-X_{i-1}^n(t)}{\sqrt{n}},\ i=2,\dots,K.
\]
In the paper it is shown that when \(b<aK\) the limiting diffusion for \(\hat Z^n\) is positive recurrent (stable regime), while when \(b>aK\) the full system is unstable but the upper-gap subvector \(\hat Z_c^n=(\hat Z_2^n,\dots,\hat Z_K^n)\) has a well-defined long-time limiting distribution (unstable regime).

problem (critical case): Analyze the long-time behavior of the MJSQ system in the critical parameter case
\[b=aK\quad(\text{equivalently }\upsilon=0).\]
In particular, determine whether the gap process \(\hat Z_c^n(t)\) admits a nontrivial limiting distribution as \(t\to\infty\) (for each fixed large \(n\)), and if so identify its limit as \(n\to\infty\). Additionally, characterize the scaling and asymptotic growth (if any) of the shortest-queue component \(\hat Z_1^n(t)\) and quantify the boundary (reflection-at-zero) effect at this criticality.

open_problem_quote: Lastly, we note that although the case \(b = aK\) is not investigated here, we believe that the average long time behavior of the corresponding MJSQ system should resemble the unstable case (Theorem 3.7) where \(b > aK\). This is because, although the lowest queue will become empty infinitely often, the time spent at zero will be small and thus the average boundary effect of the origin will diminish over time.
